title: Everything You Should Know Before Mythos Arrives | Nebulock, Damien Lewke
summary: Damien Lewke, founder of Nebulock, argues that AI has dramatically reduced the cost and complexity of launching cyberattacks, requiring defenders to shift from reactive alerting to proactive threat hunting. He advocates for an "assume breach" posture and identifies three behavioral signals that reveal attacker presence in networks.
writeup: Damien Lewke, founder of Nebulock, makes the case that AI has lowered the cost and complexity of launching cyberattacks, which forces defenders to move from reactive alerting toward proactive threat hunting. The argument centers on an "assume breach" posture: rather than treating a compromise as unlikely, defenders should operate as though attackers are already inside the network. Lewke points to three behavioral signals that reveal attacker presence and frames the discussion around the cyber kill chain. The takeaway is a shift in defensive strategy driven by AI-enabled offense.
